user_query:
I'm the CFO of NimbusAI (B2B SaaS). We need a board-ready annual report for fiscal year 2025 built from our raw datasets. Please read every file under input/ and produce two deliverables under output/.

Input files to use:
- input/revenue.csv — 2025 quarterly revenue by segment (Core SaaS, Enterprise Add-ons, Services)
- input/expenses.csv — 2025 quarterly expenses by category (COGS, Payroll, SGA, R&D)
- input/customers.json — customer metrics (total_customers_start, total_customers_end, logo_churn_pct, nrr_pct, nps, arr_start, arr_end, arpa_monthly, cac_per_customer, monthly_churn_rate)
- input/headcount.csv — start_of_year and end_of_year headcount by department
- input/milestones.md — key 2025 milestones and notes
- input/last_year_summary.json — 2024 totals for YoY comparison (revenue and any provided metrics)
- input/cash_position.json — starting_cash_2025, ending_cash_2025 (use for net burn)

Deliverables:
1) Write a board-ready annual report to output/nimbusai_annual_report_2025.md with these sections and clear headers:
   - Executive Summary (1 page feel)
   - Financial Performance (include a table showing 2025 vs 2024 for Revenue, Gross Margin %, EBITDA, EBITDA Margin %, and Cash Position; show revenue breakdown by segment; include cost structure analysis and commentary)
   - Operational Review (customer health metrics with churn, NRR, NPS, CAC payback, LTV; team metrics including revenue per employee and headcount dynamics)
   - Strategic Highlights (top wins and market position) using input/milestones.md
   - Risk Register (table with at least 5 distinct risks: Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation | Status)
   - Forward Guidance (bear/base/bull scenarios with numeric revenue targets, margin guidance, and 3–5 initiatives; include short assumptions per scenario and a brief "forward-looking statements" disclaimer)

   Requirements:
   - Do not fabricate numeric data. Base calculations strictly on input files. If a metric is not derivable, mark it as N/A and briefly explain why.
   - Use stage-appropriate benchmarks for a Growth-stage SaaS business to contextualize metrics, but clearly label them as benchmarks (not our actuals).
   - Show formulas or at least one-line method notes for Gross Margin %, EBITDA, YoY Revenue Growth %, Rule of 40, Burn Multiple, CAC Payback, and LTV.
   - Maintain consistent, clean formatting and concise narrative guidance suited for a board deck.

2) Write a JSON KPI summary to output/nimbusai_kpis_2025.json with the following fields and numeric values (numbers as floats in base units USD where applicable):
   {
     "fiscal_year": 2025,
     "total_revenue": <sum from revenue.csv>,
     "total_cogs": <COGS sum>,
     "gross_margin_pct": <(revenue - COGS)/revenue * 100>,
     "ebitda": <revenue - COGS - Payroll - SGA - R&D>,
     "ebitda_margin_pct": <ebitda/revenue * 100>,
     "yoy_revenue_growth_pct": <(2025 revenue - 2024 revenue)/2024 revenue * 100>,
     "rule_of_40": <yoy_revenue_growth_pct + ebitda_margin_pct>,
     "burn_multiple": <net_burn / net_new_arr>,
     "cac_payback_months": <cac_per_customer / (arpa_monthly * gross_margin_fraction)>,
     "ltv": <arpa_monthly * gross_margin_fraction / monthly_churn_rate>,
     "revenue_per_employee": <total_revenue / ((headcount_start + headcount_end)/2)>,
     "nrr_pct": <from customers.json>,
     "churn_logo_pct": <from customers.json>,
     "nps": <from customers.json>
   }

Notes:
- Treat arpa_monthly as the monthly ARPA and monthly_churn_rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.009).
- net_burn = starting_cash_2025 - ending_cash_2025 if cash declined (use absolute value); net_new_arr = arr_end - arr_start.
- Include any assumptions and caveats in the report. Keep forward-looking statements appropriately caveated.
- Save only to the specified output/ paths.
